runTitle: Vercel Production Chrome DevTools MCP Fallback - Frontend UI/UX Wiring Polish (v8.12.91)
coveredSurfaces: production alias https://openmanager-ai.vercel.app serves v8.12.91 on landing page
landing example questions render as static text/list items, not fake clickable prompt buttons
/login loads and guest PIN dialog opens from guest mode CTA
guest PIN authentication succeeds and returns to active-system landing state
main page system stop action opens custom app Dialog and cancel keeps the system/session active
/dashboard loads with 18-server OTel snapshot, resource summary, top-5 alerts, and server cards
AI sidebar opens from dashboard header, shows AI Engine Ready status, and exposes expected tabs/input controls
AI sidebar input accepts text and enables submit button without triggering a live AI request
AI sidebar close removes the assistant dialog from the accessibility tree and leaves the hidden node non-interactive (display:none / pointer-events:none)
profile menu opens and exposes system stop, dashboard, login, and session end actions
profile system stop path opens custom app Dialog instead of native confirm
profile session end path opens custom app Dialog instead of native confirm/alert; cancel path leaves user on dashboard
browser console error/warn count remained 0 during production UI walkthrough
critical document/fetch/xhr requests returned 200; one /api/system ERR_ABORTED is the existing route-transition abort observation tracked as non-blocking debt
skippedSurfaces: Direct Playwright MCP browser session: skipped after profile lock error (Browser is already in use for /home/sky-note/.cache/ms-playwright/mcp-chrome-2a9f8ab); Chrome DevTools MCP fallback used for browser QA
Live AI conversation/generation queue label: skipped to avoid unnecessary LLM/provider quota use; local component tests and production input activation covered the UI wiring risk
OAuth login providers: skipped because guest PIN auth covers the release-facing access path for this UI change
mobile viewport regression: skipped because this targeted run focused on desktop interaction wiring after already-passed local component coverage
Cloud Run admin observability endpoints: skipped because no observability or AI Engine contract behavior changed

expertAssessments.rationale: Targeted browser walkthrough exercised the changed UI wiring on Vercel production, while Playwright MCP profile lock was documented and substituted with Chrome DevTools MCP evidence.
Vercel deployment dpl_g7Zfcag1nht2qUvsEPYJRjQoUGrq is Ready, production alias serves v8.12.91, and post-QA Vercel usage remains billed 0.0000 USD.
AI backend behavior was not changed; the checked AI scope was limited to sidebar open/close, health badge, and input/submit UI state without spending live LLM quota.

usageChecks.summary: Current billing period 2026-06-01T07:00:00.000Z..2026-06-05T02:00:02.693Z; effective 1.9358 USD, billed 0.0000 USD, chargeCount 1827. No unexpected billed usage observed after production QA.

artifacts.label: Production dashboard after UI/UX wiring walkthrough
artifacts.note: Captured via Chrome DevTools MCP after Playwright MCP profile lock prevented a direct Playwright browser session.

completedImprovements.title: v8.12.91 production verification for frontend UI/UX wiring polish
completedImprovements.note: Custom dialogs, static landing prompts, AI sidebar open/close, input activation, guest login, dashboard rendering, console/network checks passed on Vercel production.

notes: Playwright MCP direct run failed before navigation with profile lock: Browser is already in use for /home/sky-note/.cache/ms-playwright/mcp-chrome-2a9f8ab, use --isolated to run multiple instances of the same browser.
Chrome DevTools MCP fallback verified the same Vercel production URL and saved durable screenshot evidence.
No live AI prompt was submitted during this run to preserve free-tier provider quota; the production AI sidebar health and input activation paths were still exercised.
